changes.reason: Keep PHP in sync with TSX — replace 'block' with 'flex items-center' for vertical centering

decisions.decision: Replace 'block' display with 'flex items-center' on logo container elements to vertically center logo content
decisions.evidence: User explicitly requested: 'Make the logo container center its content vertically (e.g., make it flex with items-center)'

notes: Both TSX elements (ImageDropUploader and <a>) share the same className string — the change is identical for both.

The 'self-stretch' class is retained so the container still stretches to the height of the parent flex row (items-stretch on the nav bar inner div). Combined with 'flex items-center', the logo image will be vertically centered within that stretched height.
The parent div (nav bar inner) uses 'flex items-stretch', so self-stretch on the logo <a> gives it the full row height, and items-center inside it centers the logo vertically.
